---
title: "Part 3: Working with large-scale data"
subtitle: "Intro to Computational Studies in Education and the Social Sciences"
author: "Nathan Alexander, PhD"
institute: 
  - "School of Education"
  - "Center for Applied Data Science and Analytics"
format: 
  html: default
  revealjs:
    output-file: part03-slides.html
    height: 900
    width: 1600
    smaller: false
    slide-number: c/t #< collapsted/total
    logo: "../img/howard-logo.jpg"
    footer: "[Workshop GitHub](https://github.com/quant-shop/intro-comp-educ-soc/)"
    toc: false
    echo: true
    incremental: false
geometry: margin=1.0in
editor_options:
  markdown:
    wrap: sentence
---

## Part 3: Working with Data

We'll start with a guided example using student assessment data.

- [DC Public Schools Student Assessment Results](data/DC_Public_Schools_Student_Assessment_Results.csv)

### Step 1: Load Required Packages

```{r, message=F, warning=F}
library(tidyverse)
df <- read_csv("../data/DC_Public_Schools_Student_Assessment_Results.csv")

# View the first few rows
head(df)

# Check structure
glimpse(df)

# Summary statistics
summary(df)
```
